wins.title: Brief Encounter
wins.plotSummary: Laura, a respectable married suburban housewife, meets Alec, a married doctor, by chance at a railway station, and their casual weekly meetings deepen into an intense, unconsummated romance neither intended nor can easily give up. Torn between overwhelming feeling and their unshakeable sense of duty to their spouses and children, the two are forced again and again to say goodbye in the very train station where they met. David Lean's restrained romantic tragedy, narrated through Laura's own guilty recollection, finds devastating emotion in the couple's quiet, very British repression.

wins.title: The Last Chance
wins.plotSummary: After escaping a German POW transport in northern Italy, British Lieutenant Halliday and American Sergeant Braddock join forces and flee toward neutral Switzerland, aided along the way by sympathetic civilians, partisans, and a network of refugees fleeing the collapsing Axis occupation. Their growing group, which comes to include a Polish tailor, a Belgrade factory worker, and a distraught mother separated from her husband, must cross mountains and checkpoints together to reach safety. Leopold Lindtberg's war drama, filmed with a mostly non-professional cast just after the war's end, frames the escape as a testament to collective solidarity across nationalities.

wins.title: The Lost Weekend
wins.plotSummary: Don Birnam, a chronic alcoholic writer, tricks his brother and girlfriend into leaving him alone for a weekend so he can indulge his addiction uninterrupted, spiraling almost immediately into a desperate hunt for hidden bottles and pawnshop cash for liquor. As the days blur into a hallucinatory bender marked by shame, lies, and a terrifying stint in an alcoholic ward, Don's writing ambitions and self-respect collapse entirely under the weight of his compulsion. Billy Wilder's unflinching drama follows Don's rock-bottom weekend as one of the first honest Hollywood portrayals of addiction.

wins.title: María Candelaria
wins.plotSummary: In Xochimilco, Mexico, on the eve of the Mexican Revolution, María Candelaria, a poor indigenous woman shunned by her community for being the daughter of a prostitute, struggles alongside her devoted lover Lorenzo Rafael against the town's cruelty and prejudice. When a painter convinces her to pose nude for a portrait, the resulting scandal and the community's escalating hostility push her toward a tragic reckoning. Emilio Fernández's melodrama, framed as a flashback triggered by the discovery of the painting years later, portrays María's dignity and suffering as an indictment of the racism and poverty she never escapes.

wins.title: Men Without Wings
wins.plotSummary: In Nazi-occupied Czechoslovakia after the assassination of SS official Reinhard Heydrich, young Jirka, whose family was murdered in the Lidice reprisal massacre, is consumed by grief and a need for revenge as he goes to work at a German-controlled military airport where his uncle is secretly aiding the resistance. Jirka's reckless attempt to steal a grenade gets him arrested and executed, triggering a chain of exposure that endangers the entire resistance cell, including a secretary spying from inside the Nazi command. František Čáp's drama traces the human cost of occupation and resistance through a community bound together by grief and quiet defiance.

wins.title: Neecha Nagar
wins.plotSummary: In an unnamed Indian city, the wealthy Sarkar diverts a filthy drainage channel to flow directly through the slum of the poor "Neecha Nagar" (Lowly City), triggering an outbreak of disease among its impoverished residents. As illness and desperation spread through the slum, its residents, led by the idealistic Balraj, organize a collective resistance against the callous rich who look down on them from above. Chetan Anand's expressionist social-realist drama, adapted from Maxim Gorky's "The Lower Depths," became the only Indian film ever to win the Palme d'Or, despite never receiving a theatrical release in India itself.

wins.title: Pastoral Symphony
wins.plotSummary: A middle-aged Swiss pastor adopts a blind orphan girl, Gertrude, raising her into a beautiful young woman he comes to realize, to his own shame, that he has fallen in love with, even as his adopted son Jacques falls for her too despite his own engagement. When an operation miraculously restores Gertrude's sight, she is confronted for the first time with the full reality of the tangled, compromised feelings surrounding her, and with a truth about the pastor's own moral blindness. Jean Delannoy's adaptation of André Gide's novella turns a story of religious devotion into a quiet tragedy of self-deception.

wins.title: The Red Meadows
wins.plotSummary: Awaiting execution in a Gestapo prison, young Danish resistance saboteur Michael relives the events that led to his capture: a botched arms drop, a daring escape after killing a German officer, and a dangerous plan to sabotage a weapons factory threatened by a possible informant within his own cell. As suspicion and betrayal close in around his resistance group, Michael's memories of his girlfriend Ruth and his comrades collide with the reality of his impending fate. Bodil Ipsen and Lau Lauritzen Jr.'s wartime thriller, shot just months after Denmark's liberation, honors the resistance movement's sacrifice in near-real time.

wins.title: Rome, Open City
wins.plotSummary: In Nazi-occupied Rome in 1944, Resistance leader Giorgio Manfredi evades the Gestapo with the help of fellow partisan Francesco and a courageous Catholic priest, Don Pietro, who risks his life smuggling money and messages for the underground. As the Nazis and their collaborators close in, Francesco's fiancée Pina becomes swept into the desperate fight to protect the men she loves. Roberto Rossellini's landmark neorealist drama, shot on the streets of a barely liberated Rome with a mix of professional and non-professional actors, captures the raw, unresolved trauma of occupation just as it was ending.

wins.title: Torment
wins.plotSummary: A sadistic Latin teacher nicknamed "Caligula" terrorizes his students at a Stockholm school, and secretly controls Bertha, a young shopgirl, through fear and violence, unaware that his student Jan-Erik has fallen for her too. As Jan-Erik's grades collapse and his obsession with Bertha deepens, Caligula's abuse escalates until Bertha turns up dead, with only circumstantial evidence pointing to the teacher who cannot be touched by the school's cowardly authorities. Ingmar Bergman's first produced screenplay, directed by Alf Sjöberg, uses the schoolroom tyranny as a broader indictment of institutional complicity with abuse.

wins.title: The Turning Point
wins.plotSummary: As German forces push toward an unnamed Soviet city on the Volga clearly modeled on Stalingrad, newly appointed General Muravyov rejects his predecessor's plan to retreat and instead orders an active, patient defense designed to exhaust the overconfident enemy before a decisive counterattack. Balancing the demands of command with his reunion with his wife, also serving in the army, Muravyov must out-think a German general who has underestimated the Red Army's resolve. Fridrikh Ermler's wartime drama dramatizes the strategic turning point of the Eastern Front as a triumph of patient, disciplined leadership over enemy hubris.

wins.title: The Third Man
wins.plotSummary: American pulp novelist Holly Martins arrives in post-war, Allied-occupied Vienna to take a job from his old friend Harry Lime, only to learn Lime has just died in a mysterious accident. Digging into the suspicious circumstances of the death and falling for Lime's grief-stricken girlfriend Anna, Martins uncovers a much darker truth about his friend's actual fate and criminal activities amid the ruined, zone-divided city. Carol Reed's atmospheric noir builds to a legendary confrontation in Vienna's sewers, cementing Harry Lime, and Orson Welles's performance, as one of cinema's most magnetic villains.

wins.title: Miracle in Milan
wins.plotSummary: Totò, an orphan raised with boundless optimism by a kind old woman who found him in her cabbage patch, is cast out into the cold Milanese winter after her death and settles among the poor in a shantytown on the city's outskirts. His cheerful presence transforms the ramshackle settlement into a real, thriving community, until wealthy businessmen scheming to buy the land threaten to evict everyone. Vittorio De Sica's neorealist fable tips into magical whimsy as Totò's found family fights, with the help of a literal miracle, to keep their makeshift home.

wins.title: Miss Julie
wins.plotSummary: Following a broken engagement, Miss Julie, daughter of a Swedish count, defies convention by joining her family's servants at their Midsummer's Eve ball and pursuing a reckless flirtation with the ambitious footman Jean. Their one desperate night together, charged with class tension and mutual resentment, spirals into fantasies of escaping together to Italy before the harsh realities of money, status, and shame close back in. Alf Sjöberg's adaptation of Strindberg's play strips away romantic illusion to expose the cruelty underlying the pair's brief transgression.

wins.title: Othello
wins.plotSummary: Orson Welles's adaptation of Shakespeare's tragedy follows the Moorish general Othello as his ensign Iago, embittered at being passed over for promotion, methodically poisons his mind with false suspicions about his wife Desdemona's fidelity. Shot over three grueling years across Morocco, Venice, and Italy on a shoestring budget, the film compresses the play's jealousy and manipulation into a lean, shadow-drenched fever dream. Welles frames the tragedy with Othello and Desdemona's funeral, building the entire film as a flashback toward its already-known, devastating conclusion.

wins.title: Two Cents Worth of Hope
wins.plotSummary: Returning from military service to his impoverished southern Italian hometown, young Antonio Catalano struggles to support his widowed mother and five sisters while courting Carmela, whose father won't allow marriage without money neither family has. Antonio cycles through one failed scheme after another, from a doomed bus cooperative to secret night work for a communist group that costs him his job as church sacristan. Renato Castellani's neorealist comedy-drama finds stubborn hope in Antonio's refusal to give up on love or dignity despite postwar Italy's grinding poverty.

wins.title: The Wages of Fear
wins.plotSummary: Stranded in the poverty-stricken South American town of Las Piedras, four desperate European expatriates take a suicidal job driving two trucks loaded with unstable nitroglycerin over treacherous mountain roads to cap a raging oil well fire. Every pothole and misstep threatens instant death, and the grueling journey strips away each man's bravado, exposing the fear and desperation that drove them to accept the job in the first place. Henri-Georges Clouzot's thriller wrings unbearable tension from the drive itself, building to a finale as bleakly ironic as anything in noir cinema.

wins.title: Gate of Hell
wins.plotSummary: During a 12th-century samurai rebellion, warrior Endō Morito is assigned to protect the noblewoman Kesa, who has risked her life impersonating her lord's sister to help the real family escape. Having fallen obsessively in love with Kesa during their ordeal, Morito is devastated to learn she is already married, and demands from his lord the one reward he desires: her hand. Teinosuke Kinugasa's film, Japan's first color feature shown internationally, follows Morito's dangerous fixation as it threatens to destroy Kesa's marriage and both their lives.

wins.title: Marty
wins.plotSummary: Marty Piletti, a good-natured but lonely 34-year-old Bronx butcher, is constantly pressured by his mother and friends to find a wife, having resigned himself to bachelorhood after years of romantic disappointment. At a dance hall one Saturday night, he meets Clara, a plain, socially awkward schoolteacher freshly abandoned by her blind date, and the two spend an unexpectedly tender evening together. Delbert Mann's intimate drama follows Marty's struggle to defy his friends' mockery and his own self-doubt to pursue the one connection that finally feels genuine.

wins.title: The Silent World
wins.plotSummary: Jacques Cousteau and his crew aboard the research vessel Calypso spend two years diving through the Mediterranean, Red Sea, and Indian Ocean, capturing some of the first color underwater footage of marine life ever filmed. The documentary marvels at coral reefs, shipwrecks, and exotic sea creatures while also candidly showing the crew's own rough treatment of the ocean they're documenting, including a shark cull and the use of dynamite to survey a reef. Co-directed by Cousteau and a young Louis Malle, the film became the first documentary ever to win the Palme d'Or.

wins.title: Friendly Persuasion
wins.plotSummary: Jess Birdwell, a Quaker farmer in Civil War-era Indiana, balances his pacifist faith with a private love of music and horse racing, while his devout wife Eliza holds firmly to their community's peace testimony. As the war closes in and armed raiders threaten their region, the Birdwell family is torn between their religious convictions and the pull to defend their home and each other. William Wyler's drama follows each family member's individual reckoning with violence, testing whether faith can survive contact with war.

wins.title: The Cranes Are Flying
wins.plotSummary: In Moscow on the day Germany invades the Soviet Union, young lovers Veronika and Boris are separated when Boris volunteers for the front, leaving Veronika to face the war's devastation largely alone. After her parents are killed in an air raid, Veronika is taken in by Boris's family, where his cousin Mark's obsessive pursuit of her curdles into an act of violence that upends her life. Mikhail Kalatozov's visually daring war drama follows Veronika's endurance through loss, guilt, and a long, faithful wait for a return that hangs in painful uncertainty.

wins.title: Black Orpheus
wins.plotSummary: Setting the Greek myth of Orpheus and Eurydice amid the samba and Carnaval festivities of a Rio de Janeiro favela, the film follows trolley driver Orfeu, engaged to the fiery Mira, as he falls instantly for newcomer Eurydice, who is being stalked by a mysterious figure representing Death. Their brief, ecstatic romance unfolds against the exuberant chaos of Carnaval, even as Eurydice's pursuer closes in with tragic inevitability. Marcel Camus's film, propelled by a landmark bossa nova soundtrack, transforms ancient tragedy into vibrant, doomed celebration.

wins.title: La Dolce Vita
wins.plotSummary: Tabloid journalist Marcello Rubini drifts through seven days and nights of Rome's high society, chasing celebrity gossip, fleeting romances, and his own vague ambitions to be a serious writer, without ever finding real satisfaction in any of it. His restless search for meaning takes him from a starlet's midnight dip in the Trevi Fountain to his father's awkward visit to a decadent aristocratic party, each episode leaving him a little more disillusioned and detached. Federico Fellini's episodic portrait ends with Marcello, numbed by indulgence, unable to even recognize an earlier symbol of innocence calling to him from across a beach.

wins.title: The Long Absence
wins.plotSummary: Sixteen years after her husband Albert vanished during World War II, café owner Thérèse becomes convinced that an amnesiac tramp who wanders past her Puteaux pub every day is actually him, recognizing his face and mannerisms despite his claim to a different name. She enlists Albert's family to jog his memory with stories of his wartime arrest and deportation, desperate to restore the man she lost, even as doubt over his true identity lingers. Henri Colpi's drama follows Thérèse's fragile hope colliding with the impossibility of truly recovering a past that the war erased.

wins.title: Viridiana
wins.plotSummary: A young novice, about to take her final vows, is sent to visit her reclusive uncle Don Jaime, who becomes fixated on her resemblance to his late wife and drugs her in a near-assault on her last night at his estate. After Don Jaime's death leaves her his estate jointly with his illegitimate son Jorge, Viridiana abandons the convent and instead fills the mansion with beggars and vagrants, trying to build a righteous, charitable household. Luis Buñuel's scandalous satire watches her pious project collapse into chaos, as the very people she tries to save turn her home into a scene of debauchery.

wins.title: O Pagador de Promessas
wins.plotSummary: Zé do Burro, a poor Brazilian farmer, promises an Afro-Brazilian orisha that if his beloved donkey recovers from illness, he will carry a wooden cross on a 29-mile pilgrimage and donate his land to the poor. When he arrives at a Catholic church to fulfill his vow, the priest refuses to accept a promise made to a "pagan" spirit, triggering a media frenzy that recasts Zé's simple act of faith as a communist land-reform stunt and a battle over religious syncretism. Anselmo Duarte's drama follows the naive, devout Zé being pulled apart by opportunists on every side as his one honest gesture spirals out of his control.

wins.title: The Leopard
wins.plotSummary: In 1860s Sicily, aging aristocrat Don Fabrizio Corbera watches the old feudal order crumble as Garibaldi's revolutionary redshirts sweep through the island, forcing the nobility to adapt or fade into irrelevance. His opportunistic nephew Tancredi joins the insurgents while courting the beautiful, socially ambitious Angelica, a match Don Fabrizio encourages as a pragmatic union of old aristocracy and new money. Luchino Visconti's epic frames Sicily's political upheaval through the Prince's own resigned awareness that his class's power is ending, embodied in the film's famous, elegiac ballroom sequence.

wins.title: The Umbrellas of Cherbourg
wins.plotSummary: In the French coastal town of Cherbourg, young lovers Geneviève and Guy are separated when Guy is drafted into the Algerian War, leaving Geneviève pregnant and increasingly pressured by her mother to accept marriage to a wealthy, kind jeweler instead. Jacques Demy's film, with every line of dialogue sung to Michel Legrand's score, follows the couple through years of diverging paths, chance reunions, and quietly heartbreaking compromises neither fully wanted. Their eventual reunion, years later and each now married to someone else, plays out with wistful restraint rather than melodrama.

wins.title: The Knack ...and How to Get It
wins.plotSummary: Colin, a timid London schoolteacher, envies his confident, womanizing lodger Tolen's effortless success with women and seeks his advice on acquiring "the knack" of seduction. When another tenant, the eccentric Tom, moves into Colin's spare room and the shy new arrival Nancy wanders into their orbit, the three men's competing attempts to impress her spiral into a farcical journey across London hauling a birdcage-frame bed. Richard Lester's freewheeling comedy captures the anxious, giddy energy of Swinging London's sexual revolution through Colin's bumbling quest to catch up with it.

wins.title: The Birds, the Bees and the Italians
wins.plotSummary: In a small Venetian provincial town, a circle of respectable upper-middle-class merchants and professionals conceals a web of affairs, hypocrisy, and petty betrayals behind a veneer of propriety. When playboy Toni Gasparini confides a fake claim of impotence to loosen his friend Dr. Castellan's guard, the resulting gossip backfires and inadvertently helps Toni seduce the doctor's own wife, setting off a chain of small-town scandals each character scrambles to cover up. Pietro Germi's satire skewers the town's obsession with saving face over actually behaving honorably.

wins.title: A Man and a Woman
wins.plotSummary: Anne, a widowed script supervisor still grieving her stuntman husband's death on a film set, and Jean-Louis, a widowed race car driver whose wife died by suicide after his near-fatal crash, meet by chance while visiting their children at the same boarding school. Drawn together by shared loss and quiet recognition, they begin a tentative romance shadowed by the memory of the spouses they can't quite let go of. Claude Lelouch's romance, shifting fluidly between color, black-and-white, and sepia, follows the couple's hesitant attempt to build something new out of parallel grief.

wins.title: Blowup
wins.plotSummary: A fashionable, jaded London photographer idly snaps pictures of a couple in a park, only to become convinced, after developing and enlarging the images, that he has accidentally photographed a murder. As he obsessively blows up the grainy photos searching for proof, the truth of what he saw grows less certain rather than clearer, and the evidence itself seems to physically disappear. Michelangelo Antonioni's first English-language film uses the photographer's fruitless quest for certainty as a meditation on the unreliability of perception in Swinging London's mod, sensation-driven culture.

wins.title: If....
wins.plotSummary: At a traditional British boys' boarding school, nonconformist student Mick Travis and his friends chafe under the petty tyranny of the "Whips," senior prefects who terrorize and sexually exploit the younger boys with the tacit approval of an indifferent administration. As the school's casual brutality and rigid hierarchy push the boys past their breaking point, Mick and his allies arm themselves and stage an armed revolt during a school ceremony. Lindsay Anderson's surreal, black-and-white-and-color satire escalates from wry observation of institutional cruelty into a full-blown, ambiguous fantasy of violent youthful rebellion.

wins.title: M*A*S*H
wins.plotSummary: At the 4077th Mobile Army Surgical Hospital during the Korean War, new surgeons "Hawkeye" Pierce and "Duke" Forrest arrive as insubordinate, irreverent troublemakers who nonetheless prove to be brilliant combat surgeons. Robert Altman follows the unit's chaotic mix of black comedy and gruesome operating-room reality, with pranks aimed at the self-righteous Major "Hot Lips" Houlihan and her lover Frank Burns providing much of the mayhem. Beneath its Korean War setting, the film's anarchic tone doubles as commentary on the ongoing war in Vietnam.

wins.title: The Go-Between
wins.plotSummary: In the summer of 1900, twelve-year-old Leo Colston visits the country estate of a wealthy school friend and finds himself unknowingly recruited as a secret messenger between Marian, the family's daughter, and Ted Burgess, a tenant farmer she's involved with. Marian is expected to marry the aristocratic Viscount Trimingham instead, and Leo remains oblivious to the affair he's enabling until the truth comes out with devastating consequences. Told partly in flashback from Leo's old age, the film traces how one childhood summer's rigid class barriers left him permanently scarred.

wins.title: The Mattei Affair
wins.plotSummary: The film traces the career of Enrico Mattei, the postwar Italian businessman who defied pressure to sell off Italy's oil industry to American companies and instead built the state-owned energy giant Eni into a rival of the international "Seven Sisters." Told partly as flashback and partly as director Francesco Rosi's own investigation into Mattei's unexplained death in a 1962 plane crash, the film interweaves Mattei's rise with the parallel disappearance of a journalist looking into the case. Blending documentary technique with dramatization, it treats Mattei's story and its unresolved mystery as a single act of investigation.

wins.title: The Working Class Goes to Heaven
wins.originalTitle: La classe operaia va in paradiso
wins.plotSummary: Lulù Massa is a model factory worker whose efficiency management uses to justify demanding more from everyone else, making him resented by his colleagues and indifferent to both the unionists and the student radicals outside the gates. After losing a finger in a workplace accident, his outlook changes: he joins a strike, has an affair, and lets student activists move into his flat, costing him his lover and eventually his job. Rehired after the union negotiates on his behalf, Lulù returns to the assembly line no longer quite the same man, the film ending on his fog-shrouded dream of breaking through a wall alongside his co-workers.

wins.title: The Hireling
wins.plotSummary: In the years just after World War I, former sergeant-major Ledbetter is hired to chauffeur the recently widowed, depressed Lady Franklin, becoming the only person she can confide in as she slowly recovers. She meets and falls for a fellow committee member at a boys' club, the war-scarred Captain Cantrip, while Ledbetter's own feelings for her deepen unnoticed and his business and personal life quietly unravel. When Ledbetter learns that Lady Franklin and Cantrip have become engaged and that he has no place in their world, he drives back to his garage in a drunken rage and destroys it.

wins.title: Scarecrow
wins.plotSummary: Max, a short-tempered ex-convict, and Lion, a childlike former sailor, meet on the road in California and agree to become business partners in a car wash once they reach Pittsburgh, with Lion detouring to Detroit to reconcile with the mother of the son he's never met. A stint in a prison farm and a brutal assault on Lion there test and ultimately deepen their bond, with Max becoming Lion's protector as their roles shift. When they finally reach Detroit, Lion learns the woman lied about even having his child, a blow that sends him into a breakdown Max can only watch helplessly.

wins.title: The Conversation
wins.plotSummary: Harry Caul, an obsessively private surveillance expert, is hired to record a couple's conversation in a crowded San Francisco square, haunted by guilt over a past job whose recordings led to three deaths. As he pieces together the ambiguous tape, Harry becomes convinced the couple he recorded are now in danger from his own client, dragging him deeper into paranoia about what his work has set in motion. Francis Ford Coppola's thriller follows Harry's unraveling certainty about what he heard and what it means, until his obsession with privacy and guilt consumes him entirely.

wins.title: Chronicle of the Years of Fire
wins.plotSummary: Spanning six chapters from the late 1930s to 1954, the film follows Algerian peasant Ahmed as drought, exploitation, and French colonial rule push him and his village toward political awakening. Beginning with famine years under harsh colonial taxation, it tracks the growing consciousness that emerges through World War II, as Algerians are conscripted to fight for a country that denies them freedom while promising empty reforms. The chronicle builds steadily toward the eve of Algeria's War of Independence, tracing one family's radicalization as a stand-in for the nation's.

wins.title: Taxi Driver
wins.plotSummary: Vietnam veteran and chronic insomniac Travis Bickle takes a job driving a New York City cab through the night, growing more alienated and disgusted by the city's decay as his isolation deepens. His clumsy attempt at connection with campaign worker Betsy ends in humiliation, and his fixation on a child prostitute named Iris curdles his loneliness into a violent need to "save" her by force. Martin Scorsese follows Travis's descent from detached observer to armed vigilante, climaxing in a bloody rescue attempt that the city, perversely, hails as heroism.

wins.title: Padre Padrone
wins.plotSummary: Six-year-old Gavino is pulled out of school by his tyrannical father and sent to tend the family's sheep alone in the Sardinian mountains, spending the next fourteen years isolated and abused under his father's control. Compulsory military service becomes his unlikely escape route, exposing him to electronics, the Italian language, and the classical education his father always denied him. Returning home determined to attend university despite his father's threats, Gavino eventually breaks free to become an acclaimed linguist studying the very Sardinian dialect of his upbringing.

wins.title: The Tree of Wooden Clogs
wins.plotSummary: Over the course of a year in 1898, the film follows four peasant families sharing a farmhouse on a Lombard landlord's estate near Bergamo, observing their labor, faith, and small joys with unhurried, near-documentary detail. Political unrest simmers at the edges of their world, glimpsed through a communist agitator's speech and a newlywed couple's brush with political arrests in Milan, but the families remain largely absorbed in daily survival. When one father cuts down a tree to make wooden clogs for his son's schooling, the landlord evicts the family, and the others can only watch them leave and quietly reflect on their own precarious place.

wins.title: Apocalypse Now
wins.plotSummary: During the Vietnam War, Captain Willard is sent on a secret mission up a river into Cambodia to find and assassinate Colonel Kurtz, a decorated Special Forces officer who has gone rogue and established himself as a godlike warlord among a private army. As Willard's boat journeys deeper upriver, the mission exposes him and his crew to the war's escalating absurdity and horror, from an air cavalry officer's operatic helicopter assault to a chaotic USO show. Loosely adapted from Joseph Conrad's "Heart of Darkness," the film turns Willard's journey into a descent into the war's, and his own, moral abyss.

wins.title: The Tin Drum
wins.plotSummary: In the Free City of Danzig before and during World War II, Oskar Matzerath, a child of uncertain parentage born to a woman torn between her German husband and Polish lover, wills himself to stop growing at age three rather than join the adult world. Armed with a tin drum he beats furiously whenever displeased and a scream that can shatter glass, Oskar becomes a mocking, unsettling witness to his family's petty betrayals and the rise of Nazism around him. Volker Schlöndorff's adaptation of Günter Grass's novel uses Oskar's arrested body and clear-eyed cruelty to view the era's horrors from the perspective of someone who refuses, in every sense, to grow up.

wins.title: All That Jazz
wins.plotSummary: Joe Gideon, a workaholic director-choreographer modeled on Bob Fosse himself, burns through amphetamines and cigarettes while simultaneously staging an ambitious Broadway musical and editing a film about a stand-up comedian, all while narrating his own life to an imagined Angel of Death. As the strain of overwork, failed relationships, and self-destruction mounts, Joe's grip on reality slips into elaborate musical fantasy sequences that blur rehearsal, memory, and hallucination. The film builds to Joe's own staged, fantastical death, framed as the final show he choreographs for himself.

wins.title: Kagemusha
wins.plotSummary: In feudal Japan, the dying warlord Takeda Shingen orders his clan to conceal his death and use a petty thief who closely resembles him as a decoy to deter rival lords. The thief is trained to impersonate Shingen convincingly enough to fool allies and enemies alike, gradually growing into the role. When he is exposed after falling from Shingen's horse, revealing he lacks the warlord's battle scars, he is cast out in disgrace. The film ends with the catastrophic Battle of Nagashino, where the Takeda cavalry is destroyed by Oda Nobunaga's riflemen.

wins.title: Man of Iron
wins.plotSummary: A cynical, alcoholic radio journalist named Winkel is sent by Communist Party officials to the striking Gdańsk shipyard to dig up compromising material on Maciej Tomczyk, a leading Solidarity activist. As Winkel investigates Tomczyk's past and his marriage to the imprisoned filmmaker Agnieszka, he becomes increasingly sympathetic to the workers' cause instead of discrediting it. The film incorporates real newsreel footage from the 1980 strikes, including appearances by Lech Wałęsa. It closes with the government's negotiators reaching an agreement with the strikers, freeing Tomczyk and Agnieszka to reunite.

wins.title: Missing
wins.plotSummary: After a military coup in an unnamed South American country (based on Chile), American journalist Charles Horman disappears. His conservative father Ed travels there and, together with Charles's wife Beth, tries to track him down. Their search reveals that the U.S. embassy is stonewalling them and that American officials quietly supported the coup that toppled the elected government. Ed eventually learns that Charles was executed by the junta with the knowledge of U.S. officials, and returns home only after his son's body is finally released.

wins.title: Yol
wins.plotSummary: Five prisoners in Turkey are granted a week's leave from jail to visit their homes and families in the aftermath of the 1980 military coup. One returns to find his wife has turned to prostitution to survive and dies trying to save her from freezing in the snow. Another is shot alongside his wife on a train after her family discovers their affair. A third abandons a planned escape across the Syrian border when his brother is killed, forcing him to take on the family's honor-bound obligations instead.

wins.title: The Ballad of Narayama
wins.plotSummary: In a poor 19th-century Japanese mountain village, tradition dictates that the elderly must be carried to a remote peak to die once they turn 70, so as not to burden the community. Orin, a healthy 69-year-old widow, spends her final year putting her family's affairs in order and arranging a new wife for her widowed son before willingly preparing for her own journey up the mountain. The film intercuts the villagers' harsh, often brutal struggle for survival with unflinching footage of the natural world around them.

wins.title: Paris, Texas
wins.plotSummary: Travis Henderson wanders out of the Texas desert after vanishing without explanation four years earlier, and is found and reunited with his brother Walt in Los Angeles. There he reconnects with his young son Hunter, who Walt and his wife have been raising, and slowly resolves to find the boy's mother, Jane. Travis traces Jane to Houston, where she works at a peep show, and speaks to her through a one-way mirror, finally explaining what drove them apart. He reunites Hunter with Jane before walking away alone.

wins.title: When Father Was Away on Business
wins.plotSummary: In Tito's Yugoslavia in 1950, six-year-old Malik cannot understand why his father Meša, a Communist official, has suddenly been sent away. In truth, Meša has been informed on by his own brother-in-law over an indiscreet remark about a political cartoon and is serving time in a labor camp. Once the family is allowed to join him near his camp, Malik develops a childhood romance with a local girl while gradually witnessing his father's infidelity and the strained ties within his own family. The story unfolds entirely through Malik's uncomprehending, matter-of-fact perspective on the adult world around him.
